evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: maintaining warehouse health; consistency and documentation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: documentation and warehouse health emphasis (rigour) balanced with infrastructure focus.
evidence.domain_risk: HealthTech (orthodontics clinics); high domain risk due to healthcare/patient data.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: High: regulated healthcare sector with patient data implies HIPAA/GDPR compliance requirements.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: dbt in responsibilities, warehouse infrastructure, growing function with integration needs.
evidence.jd_authorship: Designing dbt data models ensuring consistency and documentation; integrating new sources
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: named tools (dbt, Airflow, Airbyte) with specific methodology (consistency, documentation).
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Product-focused: gathering requirements and translating business needs into technical solutions.
evidence.autonomy_level: managing end-to-end projects; translating business needs into technical solutions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Mixed: project ownership and translation authority plus some execution under direction.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI user: candidate expected to use AI tools to accelerate their coding and development work.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing or data quality signals in available JD content.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: operational reliability is a concern but not compliance/regulatory primary driver.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: integrating new sources and maintaining warehouse health
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Fix/scale: task is to maintain and scale existing warehouse, not build greenfield.
evidence.loss_aversion: Maintaining warehouse health — preventing infrastructure breakage, not regulatory exposure.
